regions.us-ports: Today Miami is under a Heat Advisory with heat index values near 105 F while winds are ESE 5kt, skies are clear, and rain probability is less than 10%, creating a heightened heat risk for outdoor shore excursions and embarkation staging. Over the next 24–48 hours Port Everglades and Port Canaveral will see Miami Day 1 and Day 2 conditions of ESE 8kt and ESE 9kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, which supports scheduled embarkation and transfer operations. Beyond 48 hours the Miami Day 3 forecast tightens to ESE 16kt with overcast skies and a 10% rain probability, and the increased ESE wind to 16kt could affect tendering and mooring procedures at Tampa Bay and nearby ports. Separate Heat Advisories are also in effect for Los Angeles with heat index values near 105 F and for San Diego while Miami’s ESE 5kt, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability remain the operational baseline for today's embarkation actions.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today ESE 5kt, clear skies, less than 10% rain probability are in effect for Nassau, and those ESE 5kt, clear skies conditions support embarkation, disembarkation and shore excursions at Nassau, Freeport and Bimini. Day 1 (next 24 hours) calls for ESE 11kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, with steadier ESE 11kt winds and overcast skies expected to require routine deck and boarding adjustments in Key West, the Berry Islands and Freeport. Day 2 (24–48 hours) indicates E 9kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability across Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios and Falmouth, where lighter E 9kt winds and overcast conditions should permit scheduled tenders and open-air excursions. Beyond 48 hours on Day 3 the forecast shows ESE 17kt, thunderstorms, 13% rain probability, with ESE 17kt gustier winds and thunderstorms that could complicate tendering and gangway operations at Bimini, the Berry Islands and Grand Cayman.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today significant weather: thunderstorms with a 49% rain probability and ESE 11 kt winds with partly cloudy skies in San Juan may affect port operations in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten. Current ESE 11 kt winds and the 49% rain probability in San Juan mean embarkation and outdoor shore excursions in San Juan and St. Thomas may be affected by thunderstorms and intermittent showers under partly cloudy skies. Through the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly E 16 kt winds with thunderstorms and a 49% rain probability are forecast for Day 1 in San Juan and may affect port calls at San Juan and St. Croix, then E 16 kt drizzle with a 25% rain probability is forecast for Day 2 in San Juan for St. Kitts and Antigua. Beyond 48 hours the Day 3 forecast for San Juan shows E 17 kt winds, overcast skies, and less than 10% rain probability, with sustained easterly E 17 kt winds to be factored into ship maneuvering and tender operations at St. Maarten and Antigua.
regions.western-caribbean: Today elevated heat is forecast for Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City with heat index values reaching 100F today, 102F on day two, and 101F on day three, and Cozumel current conditions are ENE 7kt, partly cloudy, 11% rain probability. Today port and shore-excursion managers in Cozumel should plan operations around the elevated heat while Cozumel's near-term outlook shows ENE 9kt, drizzle, 11% rain probability. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Roatan and Belize City will remain under elevated heat with a peak heat index near 102F while Cozumel's 24-48 hour forecast is ENE 9kt, drizzle, 15% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours vessels and excursion teams should note persistent elevated heat with a shift to E 11kt, drizzle, 25% rain probability in Cozumel as Costa Maya and Roatan continue to be affected.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today Elevated winds 21kt are posted for Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena with elevated winds of 22kt on Day 2 and Day 3; Aruba’s Day 1 forecast shows E 21kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability. For port operations today, current conditions at Aruba are E 19kt and partly cloudy with less than 10% rain probability, and the 19–21 kt east wind will require adjusted gangway and tender procedures at Aruba and Cartagena. Over the next 24–48 hours Curaçao and Bonaire are expected to experience E 22kt with drizzle and a 16% rain probability on Day 2, so exposed shore excursions and docking schedules should account for sustained 22 kt east winds. Beyond 48 hours the trend continues with Day 3 showing E 22kt and drizzle with a 24% rain probability for Aruba and Cartagena, indicating continued elevated east winds of 22 kt for embarkation, disembarkation, and excursion timing.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today SIGNIFICANT WEATHER TODAY: thunderstorms, 59% rain probability | SIGNIFICANT WEATHER DAY 2: rain showers, 34% rain probability, with Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique and Dominica identified as most affected; current conditions at Barbados are ENE 12kt and overcast and these conditions may affect tendering and outdoor shore excursions. The Day 1 forecast for Barbados is E 16kt with thunderstorms and 59% rain probability and may affect embarkation and disembarkation procedures at Barbados and Martinique. Over the next 24 to 48 hours the Day 2 forecast shows E 16kt with rain showers and 34% rain probability and this may affect scheduled outdoor excursions and tender operations at Antigua, St. Kitts and St. Maarten. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 guidance indicates ENE 16kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability for Grenada, St. Vincent and Barbados.
regions.los-angeles: Today Los Angeles / San Pedro reports WSW 4kt, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability, allowing on-time embarkation and disembarkation at Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach with minimal wind-related tendering concerns. For the next 24 hours (Day 1) winds increase to WSW 11kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, keeping docking maneuvers and gangway operations manageable for Long Beach and Catalina Island calls. Between 24 and 48 hours (Day 2) the forecast shifts to SSE 8kt and partly cloudy with less than 10% rain probability, which will be the expected conditions for Marina del Rey and Catalina Island shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours (Day 3) a continued SSE 9kt breeze with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability indicates southeasterly light winds for Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach that should not compromise scheduled deck activities or port movements.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today ELEVATED HEAT: Heat Index reaching 104F is forecast for passengers ashore in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz, with current conditions WNW 7kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability. Today’s embarkation and disembarkation at Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo will operate under light WNW 7kt winds and partly cloudy skies with less than 10% rain probability, so heat precautions are recommended for outdoor staging. Over the next 24-48 hours winds shift to W 9kt on Day 1 and WSW 9kt on Day 2 with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, affecting scheduled operations and shore excursions in San Jose del Cabo and La Paz as crews adjust to the W to WSW wind trend. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 shows WSW 8kt and partly cloudy conditions with 14% rain probability, and the continued WSW 8kt wind may alter tendering windows in La Paz and Cabo San Lucas.
regions.mazatlan: Today elevated heat is forecast with heat index reaching 103F and an elevated rain chance of 44% (drizzle); current conditions at Mazatlan are E 2kt and partly cloudy with 44% rain probability, which may affect embarkation and shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Day 2 calls for SE 7kt with thunderstorms and 52% rain probability and these conditions may affect shore excursions and tendering at Mazatlan and Topolobampo. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 is forecast NW 7kt and overcast with 27% rain probability and elevated heat with heat index reaching 101F, which should be considered when scheduling outdoor activities for Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today ELEVATED HEAT DAY 2: Heat Index reaching 102F for Puerto Vallarta and Punta Mita; current conditions are NNE 4kt with overcast skies and 33% rain probability. Over the course of today winds are forecast E 8kt with drizzle and a 33% rain probability, which may affect shore excursions and tendering operations at Puerto Vallarta and Punta Mita. In the next 24 to 48 hours the forecast is ESE 7kt with drizzle and a 34% rain probability, which may affect scheduled shore excursions at Yelapa and Chacala. regions.western-mediterranean: Today Barcelona reports WSW 2kt winds, partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting routine embarkation, tender operations and scheduled shore excursions at the Barcelona berth. Over the next 24 hours Day 1 forecasts SSW 8kt winds, partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability in Barcelona; ships calling Valencia and Palma de Mallorca during that window should expect SSW 8kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability conditions for gangway and excursion coordination. From 24 to 48 hours Day 2 shows S 8kt winds, partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability in Barcelona, a profile that will be relevant for Ibiza and Malaga arrivals when planning tendering and dock operations. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 shifts to E 8kt winds with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability in Barcelona; Cadiz, Lisbon and Gibraltar calls should note the wind-direction change to E 8kt and overcast conditions when arranging on-deck activities and berthing alignment.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today SSW 5kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability; this light SSW wind and partly cloudy sky permit routine embarkation and disembarkation at Naples and Civitavecchia with minimal wind constraints on gangway and tender operations. Day 1: SW 8kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability over the next 24 hours will favor smooth pilot transfers and scheduled shore excursions in Genoa, La Spezia and Livorno under light southwest flow. Day 2: SW 7kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability in the 24-48 hour window will bring overcast skies and light southwest winds that may reduce daylight contrast for walking tours in Monaco, Nice and Marseille while keeping docking conditions stable. Day 3: SSW 9kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability beyond 48 hours shows a slight increase to SSW 9kt and overcast skies that may require adjusted tender timing or approach procedures for Sardinia, Corsica, Dubrovnik and Split.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today WSW 3kt, clear skies, less than 10% rain probability; light WSW winds support routine embarkation and disembarkation at Piraeus and make tendering for Santorini and Mykonos straightforward. Next 24 hours (Day 1) calls for SSW 8kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability, so Santorini, Mykonos and Rhodes should plan for slightly stronger SSW winds during tendering and berth approaches. Between 24 and 48 hours (Day 2) the forecast is NE 6kt, overcast, 10% rain probability; vessels scheduled for Izmir, Athens and Rhodes should factor the northeasterly shift into maneuvering and shore excursion timing. Beyond 48 hours (Day 3) shows SE 7kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, indicating southeasterly winds that will be relevant for operations at Cyprus, Haifa and Alexandria when finalizing docking plans.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today: Juneau reports ESE 4kt, partly cloudy with 66% rain probability, and that 66% rain probability is expected to affect port operations and shore excursions in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway. The Day 1 forecast shows ESE 9kt with drizzle and 66% rain probability, and shipboard exterior activities and tendering are expected to be affected in Juneau and Ketchikan. Over the next 24 to 48 hours the Day 2 outlook indicates ESE 6kt with drizzle and 42% rain probability, which may affect shore excursions and disembarkation plans for Juneau and Skagway.
